title: Milestone Card v1
description: One JSON per project milestone. A milestone is a moment in time, not a thing produced — it is binary (reached or not) and often used to gate a phase, a payment, or a go/no-go decision. Tracks the planned target against a baseline so schedule variance stays visible. Deliverables and action-cards hang underneath it. Lives in <project>/milestones/.

name.description: Milestone name, phrased as a reached state: 'OrderHub live on Northwind', 'Contract signed'.

baseline_date.description: Originally baselined date. Keep it fixed when the plan was set — the gap between baseline_date and target_date IS the schedule slip, and you lose the ability to see slip if you overwrite it.

actual_date.description: Date the milestone was actually reached. Set when status=met.

status.description: planned=on track; at-risk=in danger of slipping; met=reached (set actual_date); missed=target_date passed without being reached; cancelled=no longer applicable.

gate.description: Is this a gating milestone — a go/no-go, a payment trigger, or a phase boundary that downstream work depends on? Gating milestones get surfaced more prominently.

gate_criteria.description: What must be objectively true to consider this milestone reached. Most relevant for gating milestones.

deliverables.description: Deliverable-card ids whose acceptance this milestone marks.

action_cards.description: Action-cards that must complete for this milestone to be reached.

issues.description: Issue-card ids that threaten this milestone — the milestone → issue → action chain (the issues' own action_cards[] carry the work).

updated.description: Date this card was last touched.
